questions.question: What classification defines the Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) routing protocol engine?
Which of the following routing protocols operates as an Exterior Gateway Protocol (EGP) to handle internet traffic?
What algorithm forms the mathematical engine used by OSPF to compute shortest path trees?
What metric parameter does the Routing Information Protocol (RIP) utilize to determine the best path?
What occurs when a TCP host triggers a Reset (RST) flag parameter inside its header field?
answers: The sender requests a temporary pause in traffic flow
The connection is instantly terminated due to an unrecoverable error mismatch condition
The receiver reports that a data segment contains corrupt checksum blocks
The pipeline starts a context synchronization sequence

questions.question: Which of the following traits characterizes the User Datagram Protocol (UDP) data stream?
answers: Operates exclusively inside Layer 3 routing tracks
Reliable, connection-oriented, and features heavy window sizing flows
Mandates manual packet handshakes before transmission
Connectionless, lightweight, and lacks delivery guarantees or sequencing

questions.question: What does the 'Sliding Window' mechanism accomplish inside a TCP session tracking pipeline?
answers: Cryptographic block rotations to shield payload fields
Horizontal expansion of port bounds when traffic spikes occur
Dynamic flow control to regulate how much data a sender can transmit before an ACK is required

questions.question: What flag sequence handles a standard connection teardown/closure inside TCP transport logic?
If a host configuration has an IP of 192.168.1.50 with a subnet mask of /26, how many hosts are available?
What is the primary role of the Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) inside a subnet?
answers: To assign dynamic IP configurations automatically
To discover the physical MAC address matching a known logical IP address
To forward data packages across different global public networks
